title: Formatting Error when Printing an Unpacked Array with `%h`
body:
Verilator fails to print the value of the array and instead prints `%h`. This issue is not seen when simulating with Questa.

### Example file `my_file.sv`
```
module my_file;

localparam integer unpacked_array [1:0] = '{0, 1};

$fatal(1, "0x%h", unpacked_array);

initial begin
    $finish;
end

endmodule // my_file
```

The command to execute is `verilator --top-module my_file --binary --assert -Wall -Wno-fatal --timescale 1ns/1ps --trace --trace-structs my_file.sv`. The error is shown below.
```
%Warning-USERFATAL: my_file.sv:5:1: 0x%h
                                                                                                : ... In instance my_file
    5 | $fatal(1, "0x%h", unpacked_array);
      | ^~~~~~
```

The expected behavior is that `%h` would be replaced with the contents of the array. As an example, this is the output when the same code is simulated with Questa.
```
# ** Fatal: 0x0000000000000001
#    Scope: my_file File: my_file.sv Line: 5
```
